title: paeonia × arborea var. papaveracea
intro: Paeonia × arborea var. papaveracea, also known as the Tree Peony, is a hybrid plant that combines the characteristics of Paeonia arborea and Paeonia lactiflora, and is classified as a deciduous shrub. The Tree Peony has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Paeonia × arborea var. papaveracea include relieving menstrual cramps, soothing digestive issues, and reducing anxiety and stress. Herbal preparations of the Tree Peony include tinctures, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
